texts.label: text
texts.orig: Spiral-wound PDMS (polydimethyl siloxane) membranes have organophilic (hydrophobic) characteristics, which means that the organic constituent of the feed passes preferentially through the membrane.
texts.text: Spiral-wound PDMS (polydimethyl siloxane) membranes have organophilic (hydrophobic) characteristics, which means that the organic constituent of the feed passes preferentially through the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Flat sheet, configured into spiral-wound construction for liquid pervaporation
texts.text: Flat sheet, configured into spiral-wound construction for liquid pervaporation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For gas separation, the membrane surface area per module can be 70 to 100 % higher, because thinner sheets and leaves can be used.
texts.text: For gas separation, the membrane surface area per module can be 70 to 100 % higher, because thinner sheets and leaves can be used.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Ensure that the feed is entering from the bottom part of the module
texts.text: Ensure that the feed is entering from the bottom part of the module

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Respect the flow indication arrows on the module for the correct mounting orientation
texts.text: Respect the flow indication arrows on the module for the correct mounting orientation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Do NOT pressurize permeate (vacuum) side: this will immediately destroy the membrane
texts.text: Do NOT pressurize permeate (vacuum) side: this will immediately destroy the membrane

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Cleaning in aqueous environments requires special care. This is to keep elements (more or less) free from microorganisms. In case you are processing food products, starch or other carbohydrate-containing sources like juices, wine or (soybean) milk, take extreme care to clean your element during and after experiments! Cleaning best can be done by rinsing the membranes with RO water.
texts.text: Cleaning in aqueous environments requires special care. This is to keep elements (more or less) free from microorganisms. In case you are processing food products, starch or other carbohydrate-containing sources like juices, wine or (soybean) milk, take extreme care to clean your element during and after experiments! Cleaning best can be done by rinsing the membranes with RO water.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In case of complex mixtures this may not be sufficient, and you could apply the following procedures:
texts.text: In case of complex mixtures this may not be sufficient, and you could apply the following procedures:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · 0.05 wt% sodium hydroxide at 40 °C for 30 minutes, then rinse with RO water
texts.text: 0.05 wt% sodium hydroxide at 40 °C for 30 minutes, then rinse with RO water

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rinse with hot water (max. 90 °C) to remove e.g. waxes and fats and to kill microorganisms
texts.text: Rinse with hot water (max. 90 °C) to remove e.g. waxes and fats and to kill microorganisms

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Enzymatic solutions: depending on the feed (after that rinse with RO water)
texts.text: Enzymatic solutions: depending on the feed (after that rinse with RO water)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Mild hydrogen peroxide solutions (0.5 wt%) (after that rinse with RO water)
texts.text: Mild hydrogen peroxide solutions (0.5 wt%) (after that rinse with RO water)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Avoid detergents since they may affect the surface chemistry of the membrane
texts.text: Avoid detergents since they may affect the surface chemistry of the membrane

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After use, rinse thoroughly with RO water.
texts.text: After use, rinse thoroughly with RO water.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After rinsing with clean RO water, rinse with 0.25 wt% sodium metabisulphite (Na 2 S 2 O 5 ) or with >10 wt% ethanol in water. Rinse for at least 30 min and keep the membranes wetted with this solution for storage. For prolonged storage (>2 weeks) Na 2 S 2 O 5 or ethanol should be repeated.
texts.text: After rinsing with clean RO water, rinse with 0.25 wt% sodium metabisulphite (Na 2 S 2 O 5 ) or with >10 wt% ethanol in water. Rinse for at least 30 min and keep the membranes wetted with this solution for storage. For prolonged storage (>2 weeks) Na 2 S 2 O 5 or ethanol should be repeated.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Soak element in a 0.7 wt% benzalkonium chloride in RO water solution. Glycerin should not be used for storage of these membranes.
texts.text: Soak element in a 0.7 wt% benzalkonium chloride in RO water solution. Glycerin should not be used for storage of these membranes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The membrane module should not get dry. It should be stored in the closed pressure vessel (use the supplied stoppers for that) at a temperature below 20 °C. Never keep the membrane in full sunlight.
texts.text: The membrane module should not get dry. It should be stored in the closed pressure vessel (use the supplied stoppers for that) at a temperature below 20 °C. Never keep the membrane in full sunlight.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After storage, and before re-use, carefully rinse with RO water. First permeates may have contamination or non-standard compositions and should not be used for application tests. Typically, stable operation is reached after approx. 2 hours, provided that temperature, vacuum and crossflow are under control.
texts.text: After storage, and before re-use, carefully rinse with RO water. First permeates may have contamination or non-standard compositions and should not be used for application tests. Typically, stable operation is reached after approx. 2 hours, provided that temperature, vacuum and crossflow are under control.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: EXAMPLES OF APPLICATIONS WITH SPIRAL-WOUND ORGANOPHILIC (HYDROPHOBIC) PDMS MEMBRANES
texts.text: EXAMPLES OF APPLICATIONS WITH SPIRAL-WOUND ORGANOPHILIC (HYDROPHOBIC) PDMS MEMBRANES

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Recovery or extraction of organics (in biotech or biotech-related food applications) from natural feed streams like fruit juices, wine, beer, coconut oil, essential oils (carvone, limonene) and in combination with fermentation
texts.text: Recovery or extraction of organics (in biotech or biotech-related food applications) from natural feed streams like fruit juices, wine, beer, coconut oil, essential oils (carvone, limonene) and in combination with fermentation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Removal of ethyl alcohol (and other alcohols) from wine and beer
texts.text: Removal of ethyl alcohol (and other alcohols) from wine and beer

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Upgrading of reverse osmosis permeate in juice production
texts.text: Upgrading of reverse osmosis permeate in juice production

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Combination with bioreactor in production of alcohols (ethanol, IPA, butanol), ABE (acetone, butanol, ethanol), aldehydes, flavor production as well as acid production
texts.text: Combination with bioreactor in production of alcohols (ethanol, IPA, butanol), ABE (acetone, butanol, ethanol), aldehydes, flavor production as well as acid production

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Enrichment of oxygen. This process is used as quality control in production.
texts.text: Enrichment of oxygen. This process is used as quality control in production.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ·  For gas separation, different types of leaves and nettings are used compared to liquid pervaporation, since hydraulic resistance plays a much less important role
texts.text: For gas separation, different types of leaves and nettings are used compared to liquid pervaporation, since hydraulic resistance plays a much less important role

texts.orig: •  Typical data for quality control on air:
texts.text: Typical data for quality control on air:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Typical example of pervaporation (PV) process in combination with bioreactor:
texts.text: Typical example of pervaporation (PV) process in combination with bioreactor:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The image shows the principle of a membrane reactor for continuous product recovery (alcohols, aromas). The system with pervaporation continuously produces alcohol while other systems stop when inhibiting amounts of alcohol (or another inhibitor) have been reached. In some cases, a pretreatment is necessary. PDMS membranes are stable and insensitive to fouling.
texts.text: The image shows the principle of a membrane reactor for continuous product recovery (alcohols, aromas). The system with pervaporation continuously produces alcohol while other systems stop when inhibiting amounts of alcohol (or another inhibitor) have been reached. In some cases, a pretreatment is necessary. PDMS membranes are stable and insensitive to fouling.
